seoTitle: Change Your Password in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to update your QuickBooks Online login password through the Intuit Account Manager, including two-factor verification steps.
intro: Since QuickBooks Online runs on the shared Intuit account system, updating your password touches your entire login profile across Intuit products. This guide walks through reaching the security settings, entering a new password, and completing the verification step so you stay locked in, not out.

steps.heading: Open your profile menu
steps.body: Click your round profile icon in the upper right corner of the dashboard.

steps.heading: Go to your Intuit Account
steps.body: Select Manage your Intuit Account from the dropdown to reach the external account hub.

steps.heading: Find Sign in & security
steps.body: Click the Sign in & security tab, then locate the Password row in the access grid.

steps.heading: Enter your new password
steps.body: Confirm your current password to verify your identity, then type and re-type a new secure password in the matching fields.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Click Save, then complete the two-factor verification by entering the code sent to your registered mobile device.

faq.question: Does changing my QuickBooks password affect other Intuit products?
faq.answer: Yes, because Intuit uses a unified login system, updating your password changes your credentials across all connected Intuit apps.

faq.question: What if I don't receive the two-factor code?
faq.answer: Check that your registered mobile number is current in your security settings, since the code is sent to whatever device is on file.

faq.question: How often should I update my password?
faq.answer: There's no fixed rule, but scheduling regular credential updates is a good habit for protecting sensitive financial data.

faq.question: Can I skip two-factor verification when changing my password?
faq.answer: It's part of the standard security flow for account changes, so you should expect to complete it each time you update your password.
